title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Bought a term plan? Don’t Forget to Renew it Timely
Meta description: It is important to renew your term insurance on time before it expires. The benefits you get to enjoy by doing so are aplenty.
Keywords: term insurance renewal, term insurance, term life insurance, what is term plan
A term life insurance plan can help you protect your family financially even when you’re no longer there to support them. But what is a term plan? It is basically a contract between an individual and an insurance company, where the insurer provides the individual with a life cover in exchange for periodic payments known as premiums. And in the event of the death of the insured individual, the insurer will pay a predetermined sum of money to their nominees as a death benefit. This death benefit payout can be used by the nominee to further their life goals.
That said, you don’t get to enjoy the benefits of a term plan indefinitely. In fact, such an insurance plan is valid only for a certain specific period of time. That’s why it is known as a ‘term’ insurance plan.
However, by renewing your term plan on a timely basis you can continue to enjoy its benefits without any break in the protection that your family gets. Let’s take a look at why timely renewal is necessary for a term insurance plan.
When does my Term Insurance end?
Now that you know the answer to the question, ‘what is a term plan?’, let’s take a look at when it ceases to exist.
As you’re already aware, a term life insurance plan is only valid for a certain period of time. This tenure of the plan is chosen by the policyholder at the time of purchase of the plan. And the validity of the term insurance is dependent on the tenure chosen.
For instance, let’s say that you decide to purchase a term plan. At the time of purchase, you decide to go with a tenure of 20 years. Going by this, your term insurance will automatically come to an end 20 years from now.
Why is it important to renew your Term Insurance?
So, if a term insurance plan has a limited validity, what happens once you reach the end of the tenure? It’s simple. The plan will lapse and you will cease to enjoy all the benefits listed under it.
And if something were to happen to you after your term insurance plan ends, your nominees won’t receive any death benefit payout, which can leave them financially unprotected. This is precisely why it is crucial to renew your term plan in a timely manner.
Timely term insurance renewal ensures that your plan remains active and that you continue to enjoy all the benefits listed under it. That’s not all. You can also choose to renew your term insurance for the same tenure that you chose initially at the time of purchase as well. For instance, if your initial tenure was 20 years, at the end of the period, you can choose to renew your term insurance for a further 20 years.
Benefits of Term Insurance Renewal
Renewing your term insurance plan has plenty of other benefits as well. Here’s a quick look at a few of them.
No need for a medical examination
When you purchase a term life insurance policy, insurance companies generally require you to undergo a series of medical examinations or tests. However, when it comes to term insurance renewal, you won’t have to undergo a medical exam once again since you’ve already done with it at the time of purchase itself.
You won’t have to pay higher premiums
When you fail to renew your term plan on a timely basis, it lapses automatically. And when you renew your term plan after it has lapsed, you will most likely be charged a much higher premium on account of the increase in your age.
If you develop any medical illness, disease, or ailments, the premium that you would have to pay can increase significantly. In some cases, you may even be denied coverage. However, by renewing your term plan on time, you can prevent all of this and ensure that you continue to pay affordable premiums.
No waiting period
When you purchase a fresh term plan on account of the previous one getting lapsed, insurance providers may impose an increased waiting period that can span anywhere from months to even years depending on your age and medical history.
This waiting period creates a gap in the protection and can even ultimately render the term plan useless. However, by renewing your term insurance before it lapses, you can continue to enjoy the benefits with no waiting period whatsoever.
Conclusion
As you can see clearly, timely term insurance renewal comes with several benefits. So, if you’ve already bought a term life insurance policy or are planning to do so in the near future, always remember to renew it on time before it expires.
